ErrorResponse.description:
Standardized error response with detailed information for troubleshooting.

This schema provides structured error information that enables:
1. Understanding what went wrong (error_code, message)
2. Knowing exactly how to fix it (fix_suggestion)
3. Identifying specific field issues (field_errors)
4. Finding additional help (documentation_url)

All error responses from this API follow this schema for consistency.

field_errors.message: Value must be a positive integer

example.fix_suggestion: Ensure all required fields are present and have valid values. See field_errors for details.

documentation_url.description: URL to relevant documentation for this error or endpoint
documentation_url.title: Documentation Url

error_code.description: Machine-readable error code in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE format. Use this to programmatically identify error types. Common codes: VALIDATION_ERROR, INVALID_CREDENTIALS, RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND, RATE_LIMITED, INTERNAL_ERROR, CONFIGURATION_MISSING
error_code.title: Error Code

field_errors.description: For validation errors (422), detailed list of which fields failed and why
field_errors.title: Field Errors

fix_suggestion.description: Actionable instruction that explains exactly how to fix this error and retry the request successfully. Be specific about what to change.
fix_suggestion.example: Set 'numberOfDevices' to a positive integer (e.g., 100). This field represents the number of IoT devices in your deployment.
fix_suggestion.title: Fix Suggestion

http_status.description: The HTTP status code associated with this error (400, 401, 403, 404, 422, 500, etc.)
http_status.title: Http Status

message.description: Human-readable description of what went wrong
message.example: Request validation failed: numberOfDevices must be positive
message.title: Message

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse

FieldError.description:
Describes a validation error for a specific request field.

Use this to understand exactly which field failed validation
and what values would be acceptable.

allowed_values.description: If applicable, the list of valid values for this field
allowed_values.title: Allowed Values

field.description: The name of the field that failed validation (dot notation for nested fields)
field.title: Field

message.description: Human-readable explanation of why this field failed validation
message.example: Value must be a positive integer
message.title: Message

received_value.description: The actual value that was received (stringified for display)
received_value.title: Received Value

FieldError.title: FieldError

post.summary: Validate a stored cloud connection at its purpose-specific boundaries

post.description:
**Purpose:** Validate credentials against BOTH Optimizer and Deployer APIs in parallel.

**Why two APIs?** Each service has different permission requirements:
- **Optimizer:** Checks pricing API access (e.g., AWS Pricing API, GCP Cloud Billing API)
- **Deployer:** Checks infrastructure permissions (e.g., create Lambda, Terraform apply)

**When to call:** On Step 1 Validate button click to show dual validation status.

**Response fields:**
- `valid`: Boolean - true only if BOTH APIs pass
- `optimizer`: {valid, message} - Optimizer-specific result
- `deployer`: {valid, message, permissions} - Deployer-specific result with missing permissions

example.fix_suggestion: Include 'provider' parameter with value 'aws', 'azure', or 'gcp'

post.summary: Validate against both Optimizer and Deployer APIs

post.description:
**Purpose:** Validate credentials provided in request body without saving them to database.

**When to call:** When user wants to test credentials before saving a new twin, or preview validation.

**Request body:**
- `provider`: 'aws', 'azure', or 'gcp'
- `aws`/`azure`/`gcp`: Credential object matching the provider

**Flow:**
1. Takes plaintext credentials from request body
2. Forwards to Deployer's `/permissions/verify/{provider}` endpoint
3. Returns result without storing anything

**Use case:** Validate credentials → get result → if valid, decide to save via `updateTwinConfig`.

get.description:
**Purpose:** Retrieve overview statistics for the dashboard home screen.

**When to call:** When loading the dashboard main page to populate summary cards.

**Response fields:**
- `deployed_count`: Number of twins currently in DEPLOYED state
- `draft_count`: Number of twins in DRAFT state
- `total_twins`: Total active twins (excludes INACTIVE)
- `estimated_monthly_cost`: USD sum of costs from deployed twins' cheapest paths

**Cost calculation:** Sums layer costs from each deployed twin's optimizer result that match their stored cheapest_path selection.

put.description:
**Purpose:** Calculate optimal cross-cloud distribution for a Digital Twin based on 26 parameters.

**Prerequisites:** Call `getPricingStatus` first - if `is_fresh: false`, call `refreshPricing`.

**Response includes:**
- `awsCosts`, `azureCosts`, `gcpCosts`: Per-layer cost breakdowns
- `cheapestPath`: Optimal provider selection per layer (L1-L5)
- `combinationTables`: Detailed cost analysis matrices
- `transferCosts`: Cross-cloud data transfer estimates

get.description:
**Purpose:** Expose pricing quality/freshness as typed state for UI and calculation guards.

**When to call:** Before calculation and after pricing refresh.

**Response fields per provider:**
- `state`: `fresh`, `stale`, `review_required`, `missing`, or `failed`
- `review_required`: true when user-visible review/action is needed
- `can_calculate`: true only when an exact published catalog is fresh
- `calculation_source`: immutable catalog source or `unavailable`
- `review_reasons`: structured reasons such as missing keys or failed Optimizer status

post.description:
**Purpose:** Fetch fresh pricing data from cloud provider APIs using a bound CloudConnection.

**Prerequisites:**
- AWS: Requires an AWS CloudConnection bound to the twin
- Azure: No credentials needed (uses public Retail Prices API)
- GCP: Requires a GCP CloudConnection bound to the twin

**When to call:** When `getPricingStatus` shows `is_fresh: false`.

**Query parameter:** `twin_id` - The twin whose credentials to use

get.description:
**Purpose:** Retrieve cloud provider credentials and validation status for Step 1 (Credentials) screen.

**When to call:** When loading Step 1 to show saved credentials (masked) and validation indicators.

**Response fields:**
- `aws`: Object with masked credentials, region, and `validated` boolean
- `azure`: Object with masked credentials, region, and `validated` boolean
- `gcp`: Object with masked credentials, region, and `validated` boolean
- `debug_mode`: Whether debug logging is enabled
- `highest_step_reached`: Wizard progress indicator (1-5)

**Note:** Creates empty config if none exists. Credentials are masked in response.

get.summary: Get configuration for a twin

put.description:
**Purpose:** Save cloud provider credentials and configuration for a Digital Twin.

**When to call:** When user saves credentials in Step 1, or when auto-saving on field blur.

**Request body fields:**
- `aws`: {access_key_id, secret_access_key, region, session_token(optional)}
- `azure`: {subscription_id, client_id, client_secret, tenant_id, region}
- `gcp`: {project_id, billing_account, service_account_json, region}
- `debug_mode`: Enable verbose logging for deployment
- `highest_step_reached`: Track wizard progress

**Security:** Credentials are encrypted with user+twin-specific key before storage.

**Blocked states:** Returns 400 if twin is DEPLOYED, DEPLOYING, or DESTROYING.

**Side effect:** If twin was in CONFIGURED/ERROR/DESTROYED state, regresses to DRAFT.

**Purpose:** Same as `validateCredentialsDual` but uses credentials already stored in database.

**When to call:** When user clicks Validate and credentials are masked (already saved previously).

**Path parameter:** provider = 'aws', 'azure', or 'gcp'

**Flow:**
1. Decrypts stored credentials using user+twin-specific key
2. Validates against Optimizer API (pricing permissions)
3. Validates against Deployer API (infrastructure permissions)
4. Updates `{provider}_validated` flag based on combined result

**Response:**
- `valid`: true only if BOTH APIs pass
- `optimizer`: {valid, message}
- `deployer`: {valid, message, permissions}

example.fix_suggestion: Include 'provider' parameter with value 'aws', 'azure', or 'gcp'

post.summary: Validate stored credentials against both APIs

post.description:
**Purpose:** Validate credentials that were previously saved to the twin configuration.

**When to call:** When user clicks Validate button and credentials are already saved (masked in UI).

**Path parameter:** provider = 'aws', 'azure', or 'gcp'

**Flow:**
1. Decrypts stored credentials using user+twin-specific key
2. Forwards to Deployer's `/permissions/verify/{provider}` endpoint
3. Updates `{provider}_validated` flag in database
4. Returns validation result (credentials never exposed to client)

**Response fields:**
- `valid`: Boolean - whether credentials can access required cloud resources
- `message`: Human-readable validation result
- `permissions`: List of missing permissions (if any)

get.description:
**Purpose:** Retrieve Step 3 (Deployer) configuration for a Digital Twin.

**When to call:** Loading Step 3 wizard to restore saved config fields.

**Response fields:**
- `layer_*_provider`: Selected provider per layer (aws, azure, gcp)
- `*_config`/`*_code`/`*_state_machine`: Config content per provider
- `*_validated`: Boolean validation flags per section
- `scene_glb_uploaded`: Whether 3D model file exists

**Note:** Creates empty config if none exists.

get.summary: Get deployer configuration for a twin

put.description:
**Purpose:** Save Step 3 configuration fields (function code, state machines, configs).

**When to call:** Auto-save on field blur, or explicit 'Save' button.

**Request body:** Partial update - only provided fields are modified.

**Key fields:**
- `layer_*_provider`: aws, azure, or gcp
- `*_function_code`, `*_state_machine`: Code and workflow definitions
- `*_config`: Provider-specific configuration

**Blocked states:** Returns 400 for DEPLOYED, DEPLOYING, DESTROYING twins.

**Side effect:** Regresses CONFIGURED/ERROR/DESTROYED twins to DRAFT.

**When to call:** Flutter Wizard Step 3 and Twin Overview readiness views that need predictable section/artifact state.

**Compatibility:** Existing `/config` read/write routes remain unchanged for legacy hydration and saves.

post.description:
**Purpose:** Upload and extract project.zip to auto-populate Step 3 fields.

**When to call:** User clicks 'Upload Project Zip' button in Step 3.

**Flow:**
1. Validates zip structure against optimizer's cheapest_path
2. Extracts code files, configs, and optional scene.glb
3. Returns content for UI fields (NOT persisted - BLoC handles save)

**Response fields per provider:**
- `functionCode`, `stateMachine`, `config`: Content strings
- `valid`: Boolean validation status
- `errors`: Validation errors if any

**Max size:** 100MB

- Section 3 L4: 'scene-config', 'user-config'

**Response fields:**
- `valid`: Boolean
- `errors`: Array of {field, message, line (optional)}

**Note:** Proxies to Deployer API's /validate/* endpoints.

get.description:
**Purpose:** Retrieve the full optimizer configuration including saved parameters, calculation results, and cheapest path.

**When to call:** When loading Step 2 (Optimizer) screen to restore previous calculation state.

**Response fields:**
- `params`: The canonical calculation parameters last used
- `result`: Full calculation result JSON (costs per provider/layer)
- `cheapest_path`: Optimal provider per layer (l1, l2, l3_hot, l3_cool, l3_archive, l4, l5)
- `pricing_catalog_context`: Exact immutable pricing references used
- `calculated_at`: Timestamp of last calculation

**Note:** Creates empty config if none exists.

get.description:
**Purpose:** Retrieve just the cheapest provider selection per layer - used by deployment logic.

**When to call:** When preparing deployment to determine which cloud providers to deploy to.

**Prerequisite:** Must have run `calculateOptimalDistribution` and saved via `saveOptimizerResult` first.

**Response fields:**
- `l1`: IoT layer provider (aws, azure, gcp)
- `l2`: Orchestration layer provider
- `l3_hot`, `l3_cool`, `l3_archive`: Storage layer providers
- `l4`: Analytics layer provider
- `l5`: Visualization layer provider

**Error 404:** Returned if calculation has not been run yet.

- `params`: Complete validated calculation input (numberOfDevices, hotStorageDurationInMonths, etc.)

**Behavior:** Saves params but does NOT run calculation. Call `calculateOptimalDistribution` separately.

put.description:
**Purpose:** Persist the complete optimization result after `calculateOptimalDistribution` returns.

**When to call:** Immediately after receiving successful response from `calculateOptimalDistribution`.

**Request body:**
- `params`: The parameters used for this calculation
- `result`: Full calculation response (awsCosts, azureCosts, gcpCosts, combinationTables)
- `cheapest_path`: Object with l1, l2, l3_hot, l3_cool, l3_archive, l4, l5 provider names
- `result.pricingCatalogs`: Exact references returned by the Optimizer

**Important:** This enables Step 3 (Deployer) by storing the cheapest_path used for deployment.
